text: We look for a Markov Perfect Equilibrium . Within any period t , each party J ’s optimal choice of narrative propagation will be a function of the state variable: I ν J ( σ t ). In the case of multiple equilibria, we will select a polarizing equilibrium whenever it exists. 14 The Dynamic Path The following Proposition describes the different paths that are possible in the dynamic model. After stating it, we will discuss the underlying logic with the formal proof appearing in the Appendix. 15 Proposition 3. The dynamic equilibrium depends on the state of the economy represented by α : 1. When the economy is doing well ( α ≥ α ), then in the unique Markov Perfect equilibrium, no party deploys a new narrative, and the state remains σ t = { ν c } . Along the entire dynamic path, there is convergence on regulation across both parties. 14 This is for convenience. However, the analysis yields similar results under alternative assumptions about equilibrium selection, including if we make the opposite assumption that we only pick a polarizing narrative if it is unique. 15 To simplify the analysis, we focus on the case that condition (B.1) holds, which leads to the ordering of E J functions as discussed in Section B and shown in Figure 2. The details can be found in Appendix Proposition B.1. If we did not assume condition (B.1), our analysis would be very similar, but more taxonomic, as more cases would need to be considered in terms of the order of E J functions.
